Bee Movie (2007)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $126,631,277Details
International Box Office $160,963,300
Worldwide Box Office $287,594,577
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $85,764,496
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $213,405
Total $85,977,901
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Fresh out of college, Barry the Bee finds the prospect of working with honey uninspiring. He flies outside the hive for the first time and talks to a human, breaking a cardinal rule of his species. Barry learns that humans have been stealing and eating honey for centuries, and he realizes that his true calling is to obtain justice for his kind by suing humanity for theft.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$38,021,044 (30.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.33 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$150,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,928 opening theaters/3,984 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $219,978,809

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 2nd, 2007 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: November 29th, 2007 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 11th, 2008 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for mild suggestive humor
Running Time: 90 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Plot point: Food, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Cult Movie
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Animation, Columbus 81 Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Fresh out of college, Barry the Bee finds the prospect of working with honey uninspiring. He flies outside the hive for the first time and talks to a human, breaking a cardinal rule of his species. Barry learns that humans have been stealing and eating honey for centuries, and he realizes that his true calling is to obtain justice for his kind by suing humanity for theft.
